message:
feat(codegen): Add DynamicClosure for Python scope semantics

Implements closure hoisting for functions defined in if/else branches:

- Add DynamicClosure type to runtime.zig - type-erased wrapper that
  can hold any closure struct and provides .call() method
- Add function definition collection in conditionals.zig
- Hoist function names as DynamicClosure variables before if/else
- Modify zero_capture.zig to assign to hoisted var instead of const
- Fix calls.zig to use original func name for hoisted closure calls

This fixes the pattern where Python defines different function
implementations in if/else branches and uses the function after:

    if condition:
        def get_output(): return a
    else:
        def get_output(): return b
    # get_output is visible here in Python

In Zig, const declarations are block-scoped, so we hoist the variable
declaration and use DynamicClosure for type erasure.
